content_html:
23rd July 2026

The Python Package Index (PyPI) now rejects new files being uploaded to releases that are older than 14 days. This restriction was

put in placeto prevent old and long-stable releases from being poisoned in case publishing tokens or workflows of PyPI projects were compromised. As far as we are aware this has not yet been abused, but there is no technical reason beyond that attackers weren't aware it was possible.

— Seth Larson, PyPI blog

  • A Fireside Chat with Cat and Thariq from the Claude Code team- 21st July 2026
  • Kimi K3, and what we can still learn from the pelican benchmark- 16th July 2026
